objective.subject: English
objective.statement: Organise sentences into paragraphs around a theme, using topic focus, order and simple linking language to guide the reader.
misconceptions: Starts a new paragraph after every sentence.
Keeps unrelated ideas in the same paragraph.
Chooses a topic sentence that is too narrow or does not match the paragraph.
Uses linking words without checking whether the ideas are connected.
objective.parent_explanation: Can group linked sentences into paragraphs and spot when a new idea needs a new paragraph.
objective.teacher_evidence: Organises sentences around a theme, selects suitable topic sentences, places paragraph breaks at focus shifts and explains unrelated sentence choices.

activities.title: I can group sentences into paragraphs that stay on one idea.
activities.prompt: Build one paragraph about the cave entrance.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which sentence belongs on the rainforest island?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate grouping sentences by shared idea.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Sentences in one paragraph should belong to the same idea island.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Sentence cards float towards labelled idea islands.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build one paragraph about the cave entrance.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce paragraphs as grouped sentences around a theme.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A paragraph gathers sentences that tell the reader about one connected idea.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A paragraph campfire pulls matching detail sentences into a circle.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose the best opening sentence for a paragraph about the stormy sea.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model choosing a topic sentence that matches the whole paragraph.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The topic sentence should be broad enough for all the details that follow.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A signpost is tested against three detail cards.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Place a paragraph break when the writing changes from setting to character.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise deciding where a new idea begins.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: When the focus changes, a new paragraph can help the reader.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A story path splits into setting stones and character stones.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Find the sentence that does not belong in this paragraph.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Every sentence should connect to the paragraph's main idea.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: One sentence card wobbles away from the theme magnet.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Rex made every sentence a new paragraph. Teach Rex a better way.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to secure paragraph grouping logic.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I pressed enter after every sentence. Can you help me group the ideas?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion's scattered sentence cards are grouped into themed stacks.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A group of sentences about one idea.
vocabulary.audio_script: A paragraph is a group of sentences that belong together around one idea.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A story map with sentences grouped into one glowing island.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The main idea that holds the paragraph together.
vocabulary.audio_script: The theme is the idea that each sentence in the paragraph should connect to.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A magnet labelled 'main idea' pulling matching sentence cards together.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A sentence that introduces what the paragraph is mostly about.
vocabulary.audio_script: A topic sentence tells the reader what this paragraph is mostly about.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A signpost at the start of a paragraph path.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A word or phrase that helps ideas connect.
vocabulary.audio_script: Linking words help the reader move from one idea to the next.

manipulative.purpose: Make paragraph organisation tangible by sorting, grouping and editing sentence cards around one theme.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Learners drag sentence cards to idea islands, choose topic signposts, place paragraph breaks and remove rogue sentences.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: Keyboard card movement, audio sentence playback, high-contrast group boundaries, readable line spacing and reduced-motion static grouping.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use soft idea-island colours, no flashing card effects and optional plain-paper mode.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use instant card grouping and static paragraph boundary lines.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read each sentence aloud and replay grouped paragraphs with short pauses at breaks.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use sentence chunking, dyslexia-friendly spacing and glossary popovers for topic, theme and paragraph.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Limit visible cards, reveal one decision at a time and show a clear progress trail.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow reread, undo, theme highlighting and compare-before-submit.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Treat paragraphing as organising a messy draft, with positive feedback for better grouping.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach paragraph, theme, topic sentence, detail, focus and link with icons and oral examples.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Sort sentence cards into paragraphs that share one clear idea.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: theme label + 4 matching sentences + 1 distractor sentence
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use concrete Year 4 topics and avoid culturally narrow assumptions.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose a topic sentence that introduces all the details in the paragraph.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: detail sentence set + three candidate topic sentences
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Teacher review needed for subjective best-fit choices.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Place paragraph breaks when the focus, time, place or speaker changes.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short narrative or information text + draggable break marker
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep texts brief and avoid requiring advanced dialogue punctuation.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose simple linking words or phrases that match the relationship between sentences.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two linked sentences + because/also/later/however style choices
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check that more than one answer is not equally plausible unless marked as discussion.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain paragraph grouping, topic sentence and paragraph break decisions through spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: mixed quick item from paragraph sort, topic sentence or break edit queue
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use short, decodable examples for warm-up practice.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Starts a new paragraph after every sentence.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner places breaks after each sentence in a short text.
repair_sequence: Place one boundary around each group.
Compare choppy single-sentence paragraphs with grouped paragraphs.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner groups at least two linked sentences and explains the shared idea.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Keeps unrelated ideas in the same paragraph.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner includes a distractor sentence that changes topic.
repair_sequence: Test each sentence against the theme magnet.
Move the unrelated sentence to a new group or remove it.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner removes or relocates a rogue sentence and states why it does not belong.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses a topic sentence that is too narrow or does not match the paragraph.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses a sentence that matches only one detail or a different focus.
repair_sequence: Ask what they have in common.
Choose a topic sentence broad enough for every detail.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects a topic sentence that covers all details in a new example.

feedback.companion_prompt: Rex made every sentence a new paragraph. Teach Rex a better way.
feedback.parent_summary: Can group linked sentences into paragraphs and spot when a new idea needs a new paragraph.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Organises sentences around a theme, selects suitable topic sentences, places paragraph breaks at focus shifts and explains unrelated sentence choices.
next_actions: Teacher review of subjective topic-sentence items before pilot.
Add dialogue paragraphing only after speech punctuation coverage.
Interleave with fronted adverbials and expanded noun phrases in longer writing tasks.

body.prompt: Which sentence belongs in a paragraph about rainforest animals?
choices: Bright frogs hide under wet leaves.
The bus stopped outside the museum.
My sandwich had cheese and cucumber.
The moon looked silver above the rooftops.

expected_answer.value: Bright frogs hide under wet leaves.

hints: Look for the sentence about animals.
Rainforest frogs are animals that could belong in this paragraph.
questions.explanation: The frog sentence fits the rainforest animals theme; the other sentences move to different ideas.

body.prompt: Choose the best topic sentence for a paragraph with details about crashing waves, dark clouds and a rocking boat.
choices: The sea became wild and dangerous.

expected_answer.value: The sea became wild and dangerous.

hints: Choose the sentence that can introduce all the details.
Crashing waves, dark clouds and a rocking boat all show danger at sea.
questions.explanation: The best topic sentence introduces the whole stormy sea idea, not just one detail.

body.prompt: Where should the paragraph break go when the focus changes?
body.text: The cave entrance was narrow and cold. Drops of water tapped on the rocks. Maya lifted her torch and took a careful step inside.

hints: The first two sentences describe the setting.
The last sentence moves to Maya's action.
questions.explanation: A break after the setting sentences helps the reader see the focus change from the cave to Maya.

body.prompt: Find the sentence that does not belong in a paragraph about growing vegetables.
sentences: First, we loosened the soil with a small fork.
Then we planted carrot seeds in neat rows.
The goalkeeper dived to save the shot.
By Friday, tiny green shoots had appeared.

expected_answer.value: The goalkeeper dived to save the shot.

hints: One sentence is about sport, not growing vegetables.
questions.explanation: The goalkeeper sentence belongs in a different paragraph because it changes the topic to sport.

body.prompt: Choose the best linking word: The path was slippery, ___ we walked slowly.

hints: The second idea gives a reason for walking slowly.
Use the linker that shows cause.
questions.explanation: 'Because' shows why the children walked slowly: the path was slippery.

reward_payload.explanation: Can group linked sentences into paragraphs and spot when a new idea needs a new paragraph.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Rex made every sentence a new paragraph. Teach Rex a better way.
